mapping.a_element: lattice action (improved) used to study QCD with multiple flavours
mapping.b_element: lattice field theory methods (including improved effective actions) for strongly interacting systems

mapping.a_element: thermal transition and chiral symmetry breaking in QCD
mapping.b_element: thermal properties and ground-state calculations in non-relativistic many-body systems

mapping.a_element: critical number of flavours determining the conformal window in QCD
mapping.b_element: systematic effects and phase transitions in strongly interacting few- and many-body systems

mapping.a_element: algorithms allowing for larger lattices and lower quark masses in QCD
mapping.b_element: hybrid Monte Carlo and sampling algorithms for lattice simulations

brief_justification: While both papers employ lattice field theory methods, Paper A focuses on a specific physical phenomenon (the conformal window in QCD via chiral symmetry breaking and thermal transitions), whereas Paper B is a methodological review of lattice techniques applied broadly to non-relativistic many-body systems. The core machinery—QCD's conformal window and chiral symmetry breaking—has no direct analogue in Paper B's general lattice methods for condensed matter or atomic systems.
